title: Isodon melissoides
taxonomy: Isodon melissoides is classified under the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its morphological features. The genus Isodon is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae, and within this genus, Isodon melissoides is a specific species. Isodon melissoides is native to China and is often found in mountainous regions. Its classification as a member of the Lamiaceae family reflects its shared characteristics with other plants in this family, such as its square stem and opposite leaves.
uses: Isodon melissoides is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, including China and Japan.
